instruction:
`alpha _j` is the j-th column of the fourth-order determinant D (j=1, 2, 3, 4), and D=-5, then which of the following determinants equals -10?

Options:
A. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} + {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} - {alpha _2}|
B. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} - {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} + {alpha _2}|
C. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} + {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} + {alpha _1} - {alpha _2}|
D. |{alpha _1}, {alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _1} + {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _1} + {alpha _2} + {alpha _3} + {alpha _4}|
E. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} - {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} - {alpha _1}|
F. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} - {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} + {alpha _1}|
G. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} + {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} - 2{alpha _1}|
H. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} + {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} + {alpha _1}|
I. |2{alpha _1}, 2{alpha _2}, 2{alpha _3}, 2{alpha _4}|
J. |{alpha _1} + {alpha _2}, {alpha _2} + {alpha _3}, {alpha _3} + {alpha _4}, {alpha _4} - {alpha _1}|
